head_coach.name: Kyle Shanahan
head_coach.scheme_philosophy: Wide zone run game married to play-action passing; positionless offensive versatility through heavy personnel groupings (21/12) with formational manipulation and pre-snap motion

offensive_coordinator.name: Klay Kubiak
offensive_coordinator.scheme_tree: Shanahan/Kubiak wide zone (son of Gary Kubiak, raised in the system)

defensive_coordinator.name: Raheem Morris
defensive_coordinator.scheme_tree: Tampa-2 roots (Buccaneers under Gruden/Kiffin), evolved to multiple front 3-4/odd front (Rams DC 2021-23, Falcons HC 2024-25); Vic Fangio-adjacent with heavy blitz evolution

offense.scheme_family: Shanahan wide zone / West Coast hybrid
offense.run_scheme: outside zone / wide zone primary, gap/power/counter as complements, zone read wrinkles
offense.pass_concepts: play action off wide zone (boot, nakeds, deep crossers), intermediate attacking concepts, condensed formations creating mismatches; Shanahan has evolved toward more dropback passing with Purdy while retaining play-action foundation
offense.rpo_usage: low-to-moderate; Shanahan relies more on traditional play-action than RPOs
offense.te_usage: elite receiving weapon AND inline blocker; Kittle used as move TE, flexed to slot, runs full route tree; TE is central to the offense operating from 12 and 21 personnel as a matchup nightmare
offense.rb_usage: true dual-threat bell cow; McCaffrey lines up at WR, runs routes from backfield, bumper motion to create empty formations; 102 receptions in 2025; Juszczyk at FB is a core piece enabling 21 personnel dominance

defense.base_front: multiple / transitioning to 3-4 odd front under Morris
defense.alignment: odd front (3-down linemen with stand-up OLBs in base), but will use multiple looks including 4-2-5 nickel; Morris ran 3-4 base with Rams and tite fronts with five-man front concepts; expects significant evolution from Saleh's 4-3 Wide-9
defense.edge_rush_source: primarily hands-in-dirt DE (Bosa) but Morris's scheme allows stand-up OLBs on the edge as well; Dee Winters already used as edge adjuster 78 times in 2025; expect hybrid edge/LB usage under Morris
defense.coverage_base: Cover 3 / Cover 6 primary with quarters sprinkled in; Morris ran Cover 3 at 5th-highest rate and Cover 6 at 3rd-highest rate in Atlanta; zone-heavy with simulated pressures; significantly more blitz-oriented than prior Saleh defense
defense.blitz_tendency: heavy (Morris ran 42.4% blitz rate early in Atlanta 2025, finished with 7th-highest blitz rate on passing downs; 49ers under Saleh blitzed only 15% in 2025 — expect dramatic increase under Morris)
defense.lb_role: Fred Warner is elite coverage/run hybrid WILL LB; scheme under Morris will ask LBs to be versatile — coverage, blitz, and run-fit; Dee Winters showed edge-rushing capability (78 snaps on edge); Morris values LBs who can blitz from multiple alignments
defense.safety_usage: high versatility demanded; Mustapha used as box/hybrid safety with downhill thump and slot coverage ability; Sigle emerged as box safety; Morris historically uses safeties in versatile roles (Jessie Bates played more box in Atlanta than any prior season); expect safety-as-LB/blitzer packages under Morris

te_receiving_boost.explanation: The Shanahan offense is the premier TE-as-weapon system in the NFL. George Kittle earned PFF's position-best 91.0 receiving grade in 2025 running routes from inline, slot, and flexed alignments. The 49ers run heavy 12 and 21 personnel where the TE is one of three primary pass-catchers alongside the RB and one WR. Any TE with elite receiving traits (route running, hands, YAC, speed) functions as a de facto WR2 in this offense. Kittle is 32 and suffered an Achilles tear in the 2025 playoffs — SF has clear succession need. A receiving TE drafted here gets immediate scheme-amplified value.

lb_pass_rush_boost.explanation: Morris's transition to a 3-4/odd front with heavy blitz packages (42%+ blitz rate in Atlanta) creates significant value for LBs with pass-rush traits. Dee Winters already rushed from the edge 78 times in 2025 under Saleh. Morris's scheme features simulated pressures where LBs blitz while edge rushers drop — requiring LBs who can win one-on-one as rushers. His Rams scheme explicitly used OLBs as edge rushers capable of coverage (per Mark Bullock analysis). Not a pure 3-4 OLB-as-EDGE system like Pittsburgh, but the blitz-heavy approach and hybrid fronts create clear value for pass-rushing LB archetypes.

rb_dual_threat_boost.explanation: This is the highest RB receiving-value offense in the NFL. McCaffrey led all RBs with a 91.7 PFF receiving grade, 102 receptions, and 924 receiving yards in 2025. He lines up at WR, runs option routes on LBs, and uses 'bumper' motion to create empty formations from 21 personnel. Shanahan has built his offense around a back who functions as a slot receiver. McCaffrey turns 30 in June 2026 — any RB with elite hands, route-running, and pass-protection traits receives maximum scheme amplification here.

s_hybrid_boost.explanation: Morris's scheme demands extreme safety versatility. In Atlanta, Jessie Bates played more box snaps than any season in his career. The 49ers already deployed Mustapha and Sigle as box/hybrid safeties under Saleh's big-nickel package. Morris blitzes safeties, uses them in slot coverage, and requires them to trigger downhill as run defenders. Mustapha exemplifies the archetype — box thumper with slot coverage ability and blitz capability. Morris's 42%+ blitz rate creates frequent safety blitz packages. Any safety with tackling, blitz ability, man coverage, and versatility gets a significant scheme boost.

scheme_specific_draft_notes: SF's offense is the NFL's gold standard for positionless skill players. Any TE with WR-caliber receiving traits should be valued as a top-tier weapon here — Kittle's Achilles injury and age (32) create genuine succession urgency. RBs with elite receiving profiles get a massive boost: Shanahan's system produced 102 receptions and 924 receiving yards from the RB position in 2025. On defense, Morris's arrival represents a dramatic scheme shift from Saleh's passive 4-3 Wide-9 to an aggressive, blitz-heavy multiple/3-4 odd front. This creates new value for: (1) LBs who can rush the passer from multiple alignments — not pure 3-4 OLB value, but hybrid blitz-capable LBs; (2) safeties who can play in the box, blitz, and cover the slot — Morris's scheme asks safeties to be chess pieces, not just deep coverage players. Edge rushers remain premium (Bosa, Mykel Williams), but the scheme now also values versatile tweener DL who can play 3-tech or 5-tech in different fronts. The 49ers used their first five 2025 draft picks on defense and will continue investing there. Priority traits: TE receiving, RB pass-catching, LB blitz versatility, S hybrid capability, and DL positional flexibility.
